applicability_notes: Unary elementwise negation. Golden registered as torch.neg. Supports bfloat16, float32, int32. Commonly appears in rotary embedding _rotate_half: ttnn.concat([ttnn.neg(x2), x1], dim=-1), where it replaces the torch subsequence 'torch.cat((-x2, x1), dim=-1)'. Fusable as a unary in eltwise chains (e.g. as a UnaryWithParam activation on preceding matmul/binary ops).

memory_config.shard_spec_template: {GRID} x [{SHARD_H}, {SHARD_W}] (input_shard_config; input sharded on one grid)

placement_observations.source: test_unary_reshard: ttnn.neg(ttnn_input, memory_config=output_memory_config) with sharded input and resharded output on a different grid

memory_config.shard_spec_template: {OUT_GRID} x [{SHARD_H}, {SHARD_W}] (output_memory_config; output sharded on a different grid than input — op performs implicit reshard)

placement_observations.size_descriptor: head_dim/2 slice of rotary input (x[..., d/2:])
placement_observations.source: model call site _rotate_half: ttnn.concat([ttnn.neg(x2), x1], dim=-1) — no explicit memory_config, inherits interleaved default from sliced input
